behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git rename detection semantics plus end-to-end CLI output categorization. A unit test of categorization logic can miss failures in how the tool obtains/normalizes rename status from an actual repo. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call is mentioned, and no multi-agent pipeline is present.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit baseline, rename a tracked file (`git mv`), run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report has the rename in the renamed category with zero corresponding add/delete entries for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling is a real git/CLI boundary case that often differs from mocked text diffs. The behavior explicitly requires crash-resistance and correct externally visible count output when real binary artifacts are present. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML dependence or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit baseline, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes image), invoke the CLI subprocess, assert successful exit code and that stdout report includes the binary file in counts without traceback/error.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The 'no commits yet' (unborn HEAD) case is a real git repository state that frequently fails only at real command/repo interaction boundaries. Unit-only tests can miss actual subprocess/exit/error-shape behavior. Rejected higher levels: no model output dependency and no chained agentic steps are described.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert it exits successfully and prints the defined empty report structure instead of an error message.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an external round-trip correctness check: real repository history must be read and reflected in CLI stdout counts. Mocked history adapters can hide mismatches in commit-range selection, git status interpretation, or subprocess parsing. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML judgment and no multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI subprocess over that repo, and compare report counts against independently computed ground truth from real git history queries.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged behavior depends on real git index and working-tree semantics, which are OS/repo state interactions beyond a single in-process unit boundary. Mocking can mask subtle differences in how status is collected. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not involve AI/ML outputs or multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess in both states, and assert stdout reports are identical for the same effective changes.
